title: China’s Moonshot AI Unveils Kimi Model, Threatening America’s Lead
description: China’s Moonshot AI unveiled a freely available artificial intelligence model that seemed to narrow the gap with cutting-edge offerings from U.S. tech companies.
content:
The contest between China and the United States for supremacy in artificial intelligence escalated on Friday when the Chinese start-up Moonshot AI released a new A.I. model that appeared to narrow the lead held by well-funded American competitors.

title: Chinese AI model takes US tech industry by surprise with abilities rivaling Claude and ChatGPT
description: Another powerful new artificial intelligence model from China is taking the U.S. tech industry by surprise. The Beijing-based startup Moonshot on Friday unveiled its Kimi K3, the latest release from Chinese startups whose “open-source” AI technology is making the California titans of AI sweat. The technology appears to be catching up to the best versions of Anthropic’s Claude and OpenAI’s ChatGPT. Kimi K3 topped the charts on Arena’s ranking of what it calls “front-end coding capability,” which is one measure of an AI large language model’s performance. The unveiling came shortly before Chinese President Xi Jinping’s opening address Friday to the nation’s annual World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai.
content:
Another powerful new artificial intelligence model from China took the U.S. tech industry by surprise Friday, the latest sign that Chinese startups that publicly release their “open-source” AI technology are making the California titans of AI sweat.

title: MLB restricts dugout iPad use to prevent use of AI to make decisions
description: Major League Baseball is restricting iPad usage in dugouts to prevent artificial intelligence from influencing decisions. MLB made the custom tabs inaccessible to teams starting Wednesday night, when the second half of the season started. A memo from MLB executive vice president Morgan Sword explains that the custom tab had expanded iPad use beyond its original purpose. It included recommendations on substitutions, pitch calling, and other decisions traditionally made by players and coaches. A review by the competition committee found clubs had been compliant with the regulations. MLB started allowing iPads in dugouts in 2015 and expanded their use in 2016.
content:
NEW YORK (AP) — Major League Baseball is restricting iPad usage in dugouts to prevent the tablets from running artificial intelligence to help make decisions.
